name: Suwayd ibn Amr
title: The Last Martyr
shortDescription: A warrior who fell among the dead after heavy injuries, but upon hearing of the Imam's martyrdom, rose again and fought until he was martyred.
fullStory:
Suwayd ibn Amr ibn Abi al-Muta' al-Khath'ami was a brave Shia and prominent warrior of Kufa, highly skilled in archery and combat. He always walked on the path of truth, and when he learned of Imam Hussain's (AS) uprising, he joined the Hussaini caravan in Karbala to fight alongside the son of the Prophet (PBUH).

On the day of Ashura, after heroic battles and killing many enemies, Suwayd was severely injured by multiple sword and spear blows and fell among the dead. Due to the severity of his injuries, he lost consciousness, and the Kufan soldiers assumed he was dead.

Hours passed until the great tragedy occurred, and Imam Hussain (AS) was martyred. Suddenly, while unconscious, Suwayd heard the ominous voices of the Kufans shouting: 'Hussain is killed!'. This news caused such a stir in Suwayd's soul that despite his deep wounds, his Alawi zeal boiled over, and he opened his eyes again.

Having lost his sword, he managed with great difficulty to pull out a dagger he had hidden in his boot or belt. He stood up with his bleeding body and limped towards the killers of the Imam. The Kufans, terrified at seeing him, initially retreated but then attacked him.

Suwayd, with that small dagger and wounded body, put up an epic fight until he was finally martyred by Urwa ibn Bakkar al-Tha'labi and Zayd ibn Raqad al-Juhani. Thus, Suwayd ibn Amr recorded the honor of being the 'last martyr of Karbala' (after the martyrdom of Imam Hussain) in his name, becoming a symbol of resistance until the last drop of blood.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلى سُوَيْدِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو بْنِ أبِي المُطاعِ...
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Suwayd ibn Amr who fought to his last breath after the Imam.
